criteria.label: Describe the three most common workflows (trunk-based, GitHub Flow, GitFlow) and when each fits.
criteria.example: SaaS w/ CD → trunk/GitHub Flow. Library with multiple supported versions → GitFlow.

criteria.label: Follow your team's branch-naming convention without thinking.
Tag releases with annotated tags and push them deliberately.
criteria.example: `git tag -a v1.2.0 -m "..." && git push origin v1.2.0`.

criteria.label: Configure branch protection (require PR, ≥1 review, green CI, no force-push) on important branches.
criteria.example: GitHub: Settings → Branches → Add rule for `main` with required checks.

criteria.label: Use feature flags to keep `main` deployable while merging incomplete work.
criteria.example: `if cfg.features.new_reranker: ...` keeps the new path dormant until ready.

criteria.label: Audit and evolve the workflow as the team grows; resist over-process AND under-process.
criteria.example: Yearly review: still right for our cadence? Still right for our team size? Document the answer in `CONTRIBUTING.md`.
